commentaries.author: Aquinas Study Bible
commentaries.content: The Antichrist is the horn, and the body of the beast mentioned is more than likely the Antichrist as well, even though Rome was called a beast above. The Antichrist is referred to as the beast in Revelation 13, and his body given over to be burnt in the fire is mentiond by John in Revelation 19:20. The other beasts is not to be confused with the other empires mentioned above (Babylon, Persians, Greeks) since they were destroyed centuries ago, but they are the other kings who followed the Antichrist, who were not thrown into the the Lake of Fire immediately with the Antichrist, perhaps they are given an extended time to repent.

commentaries.author: Aquinas Study Bible
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh As concerning the rest of the beasts, they had their dominion taken away: yet their lives were prolonged for a season and time. Let us consider the whole multitude of His angels, how they stand ever ready to minister to His will. Clement of Rome) books were opened: God is said to have books, which is not physical books but spiritual books, that is, the eternal memory in which the names of the elect are kept. (Apringius of Beja).

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Time. Each of the four empires had its period assigned. That of Rome attracted the prophet's attention most, and is mentioned first. (Menochius)
